# REGIME READ — 2026-06-17 (FOMC Decision Day — Pre-2pm ET)

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage:** 2 → **2** (unchanged — Mild bullish/grind holds, but the Stage 1 promotion gate just compressed to a single binary: FOMC dots in ~6 hours).
- **Total score:** +6 → **+4 (-2 delta)** — Jun 16 tech selloff and VXN reset into FOMC trimmed two lenses. Dealer Flow +2→+1 (VXN rising into event); Breadth +2→+1 (Jun 16 tech-led divergence: Dow record / Nasdaq -1.15%); other five lenses unchanged.
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — TQQQ daily close above $80:** **HELD by a thread.** Jun 16 close $80.13 — gave back $4.46 of Jun 15's $7.07 gain. Still above $80, but barely.
  - **Bullish — VXN crush back into 18–22 zone:** **NOT FIRED.** VXN now visible at **25.06–26.95** (Jun 16 close → Jun 17 intraday +3.97%). Crush partial, not complete. ARMED.
  - **Bullish — FOMC Jun 17 dot-plot held flat or dovish:** **PENDING** — decision in ~6 hours.
  - **Bullish — Iran MOU formally signed Jun 19:** **MATERIALLY DE-RISKED.** Trump and Vance **virtually signed** the agreement Jun 15 per CNN live blog. Formal Jun 19 Geneva ceremony now the public-relations capstone, not the binary risk. Strait of Hormuz reopened to all shipping Friday per Trump statement.
  - **Bearish — FOMC hawkish surprise (+2 or more dots):** **ARMED** (~6 hrs out — and base-case analyst expectation is now erasing the single March 2026 cut, which keeps the SEP modestly hawkish but pre-positioned).
  - **Bearish — Iran MOU signing collapses:** **CLEARED FURTHER** (virtual signing Jun 15 makes a Jun 19 collapse structurally harder).
  - **Bearish — Israel re-engagement before Jun 19:** **ARMED but quiet** (Israel halted attacks per Netanyahu; ceasefire holding; saber-rattling continues).
  - **Bearish — VIX back above 22 sustained:** **CLEARED with margin** (VIX 16.36).
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **CLEARED** (last print 271 bp).
  - **Bearish — TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39:** **CLEARED but margin shrunk** ($80.13 only +4.9% above 50DMA vs +10.7% on Jun 15).
- **Notable lens flips:** Dealer Flow +2 → **+1** (VXN reset higher into FOMC dilutes the post-Iran vol crush). Breadth +2 → **+1** (Jun 16 SPX -0.57% / Nasdaq -1.15% / Dow record — leadership divergence, semis profit-taking). Macro -1 unchanged. Geopolitical +2 unchanged. Cross-asset +1 unchanged. Sentiment 0 unchanged (F&G **40 vs 41.29 yesterday** — lost a tick, still Fear). Positioning 0 unchanged.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 2 — Mild bullish / grind** (with Stage 1 trigger ARMED; Stage 3 trigger ARMED).
- **Stage Anchor:** Jun 14–15, 2026 — US-Iran MOU framework + Jun 15 Trump/Vance virtual signing + Jun 15 broad rally.
- **Position:** Day 3 of Stage 2; FOMC dot plot in ~6 hours is the single biggest near-term gate. MOU formal signing Jun 19 is now ceremonial (virtual signing executed Jun 15).
- **Confidence: Medium.** Four lenses bullish or improving, three neutral-or-negative; Jun 16 tech leadership wobble + VXN reset higher confirms positioning is NOT yet in Stage-1 risk-on territory. Markets demanded protection back into the event.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 1):** FOMC SEP flat or with no dot-plot escalation, AND Warsh presser delivers "patient hold" framing (not "hike-ready"), AND TQQQ daily close back above $84.59 (Jun 15 high), AND VXN crushes through 22, AND F&G breaks 45.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 3-4):** FOMC median 2026 dot raised by 2+ (hawkish escalation flagging a hike), OR Warsh presser hints at restart of hikes, OR TQQQ daily close back below 50DMA $76.39, OR VIX back above 22 sustained, OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR Israel signals re-engagement during Geneva week, OR Trump escalation post / tariff surprise.

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment is **Stage 2 mild-bullish with the Jun 16 reset confirming positioning never fully embraced Stage 1**; Medium confidence; **the entire near-term path now compresses into the FOMC dot plot at 2pm ET and Warsh's first press conference at 2:30pm ET — six hours away.**

## Seven-Lens Scorecard
| Lens | Score | Direction | Key Data (with dates) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. Positioning | **0** | Neutral | **F&G 40 Jun 17** (down from 41.29 Jun 16, did NOT break 45 Neutral) — sentiment stalled, lagging price; NAAIM/AAII/COT/ETF flow detail not retrievable |
| 2. Dealer / Options Flow | **+1** | Bullish (compressed) | VIX **16.36 (Jun 16, +1.87%)**, **VXN 25.06 close → 26.95 intraday Jun 17** — vol pricing in event risk (4-pt gap between VIX and VXN says NDX-specific tail not yet priced out); VIX9D/VIX/VIX3M term structure, VVIX, SKEW not retrievable |
| 3. Macro / Liquidity | **-1** | Bearish | 10Y **4.44%** (rose back from 4.22–4.24% Jun 16 intraday low) — bonds gave back gains as bid faded ahead of FOMC; DXY sub-100; HY OAS **271 bp** tight; **May CPI 4.2% YoY** still hot; **FedWatch: 97% hold + ~56% odds of HIKE by year-end** — hawkish-leaning curve; binary in 6 hrs |
| 4. Breadth / Internals | **+1** | Bullish (narrowing) | **Jun 16: SPX -0.57%, Nasdaq -1.15%, Dow record** — leadership divergence; profit-taking in semis (per chip-supply-chain rumor) reverses some of Jun 15's broad-front rally; TQQQ -5.27% confirms the leveraged-NDX leg led the unwind |
| 5. Cross-Asset | **+1** | Bullish (mixed) | Brent ~$80 steady; DXY sub-100; HY OAS 271 bp tight; **Gold ~$4,358 — still anomalously high alongside risk-on equities**; the "soft dollar / hard real assets" regime undertone persists |
| 6. Sentiment | **0** | Neutral | **F&G 40 Jun 17** — sentiment did NOT confirm the Jun 15 rally; still in Fear zone; this is a divergence — price front-ran sentiment and Jun 16 brought it back to roughly even |
| 7. Geopolitical | **+2** | Bullish | **Trump + Vance virtually signed US-Iran MOU Jun 15** (CNN live blog) — material de-risking event vs the Jun 19 formal Geneva ceremony; Hormuz reopening declared by Trump; Iran-Israel ceasefire fragile but holding; Israel halted attacks; no fresh exchanges |
| **TOTAL** | **+4** | — | Net bullish but with three negative-to-neutral lenses concentrated in the event-pricing complex — markets demanded back some protection into FOMC |

## Diverging Signals
- **VXN reset higher (25.06 → 26.95) while VIX held near 16** is the cleanest tell of the day: SPX vol stays low, but NDX-specific vol is pricing in the leveraged-growth-leg tail risk to FOMC. The 10-pt VIX/VXN gap is wide; either VXN crushes after FOMC (favorable for premium retention) or VIX catches up (less favorable). The asymmetry: VXN catches up TO VIX in a dovish/neutral FOMC; VIX rips UP TO VXN in a hawkish surprise.
- **F&G 40 stalled in Fear while SPX is +0.62% on the week.** Sentiment never confirmed Jun 15's +9.12% TQQQ rally — this is either contrarian-bullish (room to grow if FOMC clears) or rally-exhaustion signal (price extended without psychological buy-in). The Jun 16 reset suggests the latter is in play near-term.
- **10Y bounced back to 4.44% from 4.22–4.24% intraday Jun 16.** The "Iran-peace bid into bonds" partially gave back as front-end positioning braced for hawkish dots. Bond market is NOT pricing in a dovish dot-plot surprise.
- **Jun 16 was "Dow record + Nasdaq -1.15%"** — classic late-cycle rotation pattern (defensives + value bid, growth + leveraged tech sold). Healthy for SPX, less so for TQQQ-relevant Nasdaq leadership.
- **Trump + Vance VIRTUAL signing Jun 15 fundamentally changes the Jun 19 Geneva risk asymmetry.** A "Geneva signing collapse" was the previous read's bear trigger; that path is now structurally harder. Israel-side escalation remains the residual tail.
- **FedWatch paradox persists:** 97% hold AND ~56% odds of HIKE by Dec. The curve is positioned for hawkish dots; *priced* hawkishness means a flat/no-revision SEP would compress shorts and lift IV-sensitive growth.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** RSI estimated cooled to **~60-65** after Jun 16's -5.27% pullback from the Jun 15 RSI-78 reading. **Healthier setup for premium-seller mean-reversion logic** (no longer overbought-extended into a binary event), but also no oversold tailwind — neutral-elevated.
- **Market structure: SIDEWAYS** — using Jun 16 close $80.13: -9.0% from 4-wk high $88.09 (above the -20% DOWNTURN threshold) AND +17.7% from 4-wk low $68.08 (below the +30% RALLY threshold of $88.50). **Rally threshold $88.50 is now +10.5% away** (further than yesterday's +4.6%). DOWNTURN threshold would require a close near $70.47 and VXN ≥25 — VXN ≥25 condition IS now satisfied (26.95), so a close below $70.47 would actively flip to DOWNTURN. Distance from spot to DOWNTURN: -12.1%.
- **Trend:** Price still above both 50DMA ($76.39, +4.9% above) and 200DMA ($68.09, +17.7% above). Two-MA uptrend intact, but the 50DMA cushion shrank from +10.7% to +4.9% in a single session — buffer thinner now.
- **Volatility & premium environment:** With **VXN visible at 25.06 close → 26.95 intraday Jun 17**, TQQQ premium is **structurally richer than Jun 15's post-rally compression** — markets reset some IV protection into FOMC. This is BENIGN for short-put credit per unit risk, but the protection is event-priced, meaning it crushes IF FOMC clears non-hawkish and the IV component drains. Premium "richness" here is event-window richness, not regime-shift richness — the distinction matters.
- **Put-selling context: CAUTIOUS** (held). Five reasons re-examined. (1) **FOMC binary in ~6 hours** with curve positioned for hawkish dots — a hawkish escalation (+2 dots / signaling a hike) cracks the rally with no protective floor for the 3x-leveraged NDX leg, while a non-hawkish dot-plot triggers an IV-crush squeeze (VXN 26.95 → ~20-22 likely) and lift toward $84-88. (2) **VXN 26.95 says event protection is priced in** — premium reward exists but is event-conditional. (3) **Jun 19 quad-witching coincident with MOU formal signing** — structurally elevated vol, pinning, and headline reflexivity all in one session 48 hours after FOMC. (4) **F&G stalled at 40, sentiment not confirming the rally** — vulnerability if FOMC adds a negative catalyst. (5) **TQQQ -5.27% Jun 16 broke the 3-day up-streak** — short-term momentum is no longer one-way. *Counter:* The macro/geopolitical regime IS materially improved vs Jun 12 (Iran framework virtually signed, broad indices still net higher week-on-week, HY OAS 271 bp tight, breadth strong on Jun 15 even with Jun 16 narrowing); RSI cooled out of overbought; and the FOMC asymmetry has shifted dovish-surprise-positive (vs Jun 12's hawkish-skewed). **CAUTIOUS verdict is unchanged: regime backdrop improved, premium event-rich, two binaries (FOMC today + quad-witch/MOU Friday) still ahead — not a free lunch yet.**

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger:** FOMC SEP shows no dot-plot escalation (median 2026 dot unchanged or removes the single cut without adding hikes) + Warsh presser strikes "patient hold" tone, AND VXN crushes through 22, AND TQQQ daily close back above $84.59, AND F&G breaks 45 within 2 sessions, AND MOU Jun 19 signing executes cleanly.
- **Bearish trigger:** FOMC median 2026 dot raised by 2+ (signaling a hike), OR Warsh presser hints at hike-ready posture, OR TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39, OR VIX back above 22 sustained, OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR Israel re-engagement signal pre-Jun 19, OR Trump escalation post (tariff or Iran walk-back) ahead of Geneva.
- **Neutralizer:** Hot Philly Fed Jun 18 manufacturing data combined with weak Initial Claims (stagflation-lite tape) breaking the "geopolitical relief = Fed comfort" narrative; OR semiconductor-supply-chain headline (NVDA, AVGO, AMD) extending Jun 16's chip selloff into a broader leadership crack.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jun 17, 2026 (TODAY, ~6 hrs):** **FOMC decision + SEP + dot plot 2pm ET; Chair Warsh's first press conference 2:30pm ET.**
- **Jun 18, 2026 (Thu):** Initial Jobless Claims; **Philly Fed Manufacturing Index** (tape-check on the Empire State -14pt June drop).
- **Jun 19, 2026 (Fri):** **US-Iran MOU formal signing in Geneva** + **June Quad-Witching Expiry** (3rd Friday — options + futures + index + single-stock).
- **Jun 22, 2026 (Mon):** First post-quad/post-MOU tape day.
- **Jun 30, 2026 (Tue):** Quarter-end rebalance (~9 trading days out).

## Expiry-Window Flag
- Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jun 17 Wed today, Jun 18 Thu, Jun 19 Fri, Jun 22 Mon, Jun 23 Tue): **FOMC decision + dot plot + first-Warsh presser Jun 17**; **Initial Claims + Philly Fed Jun 18**; **MOU formal signing Geneva + June Quad-Witching Jun 19**; first post-event tape Jun 22.
- **Window: EVENT-WINDOW** — central-bank decision, geopolitical-execution event, AND structural expiry all stacked inside the next 3 sessions. Among the densest catalyst windows in the series.

## Structural Calendar Flag (deterministic — always report)
- **Quad-witching:** Next is **Jun 19, 2026** (3rd Friday). Days-until: **2 trading days.** **WITHIN 2 SESSIONS.** Elevated volume + pinning + delta-hedge unwind dynamics dominate that session — and Jun 19 also stacks **MOU formal Geneva signing + first post-FOMC tape week-close**. Highly structurally exceptional.
- **Quarter-end rebalance:** Next is **Jun 30, 2026** (last business day of Q2). Days-until: **~9 trading days.** Not within 2 sessions, but inside the broader mid-to-late June expiry-rebalance complex.
- **Net:** **Jun 19 is structurally exceptional** — quad-witching + MOU signing + post-FOMC week-close stack in a single session. Intraday vol/volume will be structurally elevated and pinning/whipsaw behave very differently in that session vs typical Friday. Descriptive only — no trade calls.

## EVENT ALERT — Historical Playbook

- **FOMC Jun 17, 2026 (Warsh's first meeting; post-Iran-MOU partial reset; RSI back to ~60-65 on the leveraged NDX leg; curve pricing 56% odds of hike-by-Dec; VXN 26.95 event-priced).** Four named historical anchors:
  - **FOMC Jun 14–15, 2022 (CPI-shock regime, Powell):** SPX entered weak; Fed delivered hawkish 75bp + dot lift; SPX bounced +1.5% intraday on presser then **fell -5.8% over next two weeks**. Lesson: in hot-inflation regimes, even "priced" decisions can re-price via dots/tone.
  - **FOMC Sep 20–21, 2022 (post-hot CPI):** SPX entered weak, hawkish dot drove SPX -1.7% on day and -5% the following week. Pattern: **hot-PPI/CPI-into-FOMC has historically meant upward dot revisions and equity downside.**
  - **FOMC May 4, 2022 (first sustained-hike cycle, Powell):** Press-conference dovish-read drove SPX +2.99% on day, **fully reversed -3.56% the following day** as markets re-read SEP. Lesson: **first-Warsh presser carries elevated 24-72hr reversal risk on tone misreads — especially relevant here.**
  - **First-meeting-for-new-Chair precedents:** Powell Mar 2018 — SPX -0.18% on day, -1.42% next session. Yellen Mar 2014 — SPX -0.61% on a "six months after" tape-bomb phrasing. Pattern: **new-chair debut meetings carry elevated 1-2 session reversal risk independent of decision substance**, because markets re-anchor on tone/language.
  - **Updated asymmetry vs Jun 16 read:** Markets are positioned for hawkish dots (97% hold + 56% hike-by-Dec); base-case is erasure of the single March 2026 cut. **Priced hawkishness means a flat/no-revision SEP would compress shorts and lift IV-sensitive growth**, while +2-dot escalation (signaling a hike) still cracks the rally. Asymmetry remains dovish-surprise-positive, but VXN 26.95 reflects markets demanding back some protection — i.e., the dovish-surprise option is no longer free.
  - **VIX/VXN behavior:** With VIX at 16.36 and VXN at 26.95, there is meaningful **NDX-specific** room for crush on a dovish/neutral outcome (VXN to ~22 likely; TQQQ premium drains accordingly). On hawkish escalation, VIX would catch up to VXN (likely to 20-25) and VXN spikes further — vol-of-vol asymmetric.

- **US-Iran MOU formal signing Jun 19 Geneva (combined with June quad-witching).** Material change vs Jun 16 read: **Trump and Vance virtually signed Jun 15** per CNN live blog. The Jun 19 ceremony is now structurally a public-relations capstone, not the deal-execution binary. Historical signing-day anchors: **Camp David Sep 1978** (SPX +0.7% — framework already priced); **Israel-Egypt Mar 1979** (markets flat); **JCPOA Jul 14, 2015** (SPX +0.1%, oil -2.6% — framework already priced). Pattern: **formal signing is rarely the catalyst.** Combined with quad-witching, the Jun 19 session structurally amplifies any directional move via pin-unwind + delta hedging; residual risk concentrates on (a) Israel-side weekend headline (Israel halted attacks but did NOT formally acknowledge ceasefire per Netanyahu); (b) Iran walk-back of any specific MOU term; (c) Trump escalation post pre-ceremony.

- **Net alert:** Carrying short-put exposure on TQQQ through this window means accepting (1) FOMC dot-plot binary in ~6 hrs, (2) first-Warsh-presser 24-72hr reversal-risk pattern (5/2022 precedent), (3) Jun 19 quad-witching + MOU signing structural elevation, (4) thinner 50DMA cushion ($80.13 only +4.9% above the line vs +10.7% Jun 15), and (5) VXN-priced event richness that drains hard on a clean outcome but spikes harder on hawkish escalation. The dominant asymmetry has shifted favorably (Iran framework virtually executed), but two binaries still ahead and Jun 16's tech-led reset shows positioning is NOT yet in Stage-1 risk-on confirmation. Historical hot-inflation FOMC episodes saw 5-8% follow-through downside on hawkish escalation. **Descriptive only — no trade calls.**

> **Hard-rules compliance notes:**
> - Analysis only. No structures, strikes, expiries, or sizing anywhere.
> - VXN now visible at 25.06 (Jun 16 close) and 26.95 (Jun 17 intraday); previous read's "VXN data unavailable" disclaimer has been resolved with this data point.
> - VIX9D/VIX/VIX3M term structure, SKEW, VVIX, MOVE index, S&P % above 50DMA, NAAIM/AAII/COT detail, TQQQ Greeks, exact put/call ratio, and individual option chain implied vols remain unavailable from public sources and are marked accordingly.
> - TQQQ IV / 5-day EM clearly labeled as estimates with VXN-input dependence.
> - Conservative scoring applied: Dealer Flow trimmed +2→+1 to reflect VXN 26.95 reset higher; Breadth trimmed +2→+1 to reflect Jun 16 leadership divergence; F&G 40 still in Fear → Sentiment held 0; FedWatch hawkish-leaning curve → Macro held -1.
> - Jun 19 quad-witching + MOU formal signing + post-FOMC week-close stack is structurally exceptional and called out specifically.
> - Stage 2 held with Stage 1 trigger AND Stage 3 trigger both ARMED; confidence Medium because FOMC binary resolves in ~6 hours.
